prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (nucleophilic attack), f⁻ (electrophilic attack), f⁰ (radical attack) and dual descriptor for pyridine; headline figure reported here is the largest f⁺ value.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier ‘standard’, RIJK density fitting) in PySCF
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (pyridine.xyz); single-point charges on this fixed geometry — no re-optimization at DFT (DFT//user xyz)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF 2.x backend (exact version in .json `program_version` field)
Convergence & caveats: SCF for N, N+1, and N–1 states converged. The integrity checker reports:
 • fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge) — ok: true
 • fukui_charge_conservation_f–: sum(f⁻) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge) — ok: true
 • schema_header: common header present — ok: true
No entries were present in the tool’s `warnings` array (no warnings reported).

Key numerical results (Mulliken charge-based Fukui indices, atom order as in input .xyz):
  f⁺  = [0.48422572, 0.32447227, 0.14032092, 0.14039587, 0.29962626, 0.29947706, –0.14151319, –0.13368411, –0.13365905, –0.13981630, –0.13984545]
  f⁻  = [0.064122671, 0.0061877146, 0.11823139, 0.11826260, 0.11206595, 0.11200985, 0.08284851, 0.098179487, 0.09818226, 0.094960093, 0.094949479]
  f⁰  = [0.27417420, 0.16532999, 0.12927615, 0.12932924, 0.20584611, 0.20574345, –0.029332342, –0.01775231, –0.017738396, –0.022428103, –0.022447988]
  Δf  = [0.42010305, 0.31828456, 0.022089533, 0.022133276, 0.18756030, 0.18746721, –0.22436170, –0.23186359, –0.23184131, –0.23477639, –0.23479493]
Most electrophilic (highest f⁺): atom 1 (N) f⁺ = 0.48422572
Most nucleophilic (highest f⁻): atom 4 (C) f⁻ = 0.11826260

Live output & reproducibility: the skill executed via
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-TZVP --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out …/live.json
The engine streamed its run log to a `fukui_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.out` file in the working directory (path echoed at run start); the final JSON is at the location shown above. All results are computed; no experimental comparison requested.
